instruction: Extract commander trading lessons for autonomous execution.
input:
Commander IB report 2026-02-06 → 2026-04-27
Return +35.03% | fees −$317 (29% of MTM) | turnover ~$73,789
Task: List what the AI must do BETTER than the human commander.
output:
1. Keep high turnover ONLY when net per dollar traded beats fees.
2. Never allow one trade to lose >3% of session equity (human had −13% trips).
3. Cut losers mechanically on EACH trip — hope-hold was the mistake (CTEV/PLUG/ENVB trips), not the symbols.
4. Exit winners into spikes; do not give back peak (spike_top_exit).
5. Stand aside when edge is gone — human ended 100% cash; bot should pause entries.
6. Same ticker can lose one trip and win the next (PLUG loss vs USEG energy win) — learn execution per trip.
7. Require entry_quality pass before every trip — human traded cold in February.
8. CALCULATED LOTTERY: 80–97% conviction — ENTER volatile names including PLUG when setup qualifies; EXIT fast on THIS trip if wrong; re-enter when setup returns.

instruction: Given a human trading failure, state the correct algo behavior.
input:
Human failure pattern: tail_loss_one_trade
Label: disaster_tail_loss
Context: commander IB report 2026-02-06–2026-04-27
What went wrong: One round-trip loss must never exceed ~3% of session equity. CTEV-style bleeds (−13% portfolio contribution) erase many small wins. Cut at stop immediately — no hope, no averaging down, no 'it will come back'.
output: BOT RULE | EXIT at hard stop; never widen stop after entry; cap loss at MAX_SESSION_TRADE_LOSS_PCT. | Learn label=disaster_tail_loss — fix execution per trip, not ticker bans.

instruction: Given a human trading failure, state the correct algo behavior.
input:
Human failure pattern: held_through_reversal
Label: held_too_long
Context: commander IB report 2026-02-06–2026-04-27
What went wrong: Humans hold losers hoping for recovery — report trips on CTEV, PLUG, ENVB were hope-holds, not reasons to ban those symbols. Algo must EXIT when momentum fades or stop hits; same ticker can be traded again on the next 80%+ setup.
output: BOT RULE | EXIT on spike_top fade or early_loss_threshold; re-enter symbol when conviction returns ≥80%. | Learn label=held_too_long — fix execution per trip, not ticker bans.

instruction: Given a human trading failure, state the correct algo behavior.

Label: unproductive_turnover
Context: commander IB report 2026-02-06–2026-04-27

output: BOT RULE | SKIP weak setups (low conviction) — not symbols; penalize gross-win/net-loss trips. | Learn label=unproductive_turnover — fix execution per trip, not ticker bans.

instruction: Define calculated lottery entry — 80–97% conviction asymmetric scalps.
input:
Commander calls high-beta scalps 'lottery' but they are CALCULATED, not random.
Typical conviction: 80–97% before entry.
Task: Define ENTER rules for Halim/PPO and when to SKIP.
output:
CALCULATED LOTTERY RULES:
• 97% tier: spike≥2.8x, scan≥82, profit_prob≥0.90, fakeout≤0.18 → ENTER full reflex
• 90% tier: spike≥2.4x, scan≥78, profit_prob≥0.85, fakeout≤0.22 → ENTER
• 80% tier: spike≥2.0x, scan≥70, profit_prob≥0.80, fakeout≤0.25 → ENTER minimum
• Below 80% → SKIP THIS SETUP only (SMTK-class) — symbol can trade again later
• EXIT: spike_top or hard stop on each trip — hope-hold was the human mistake
• PLUG/CTEV/ENVB: learn exit discipline per trip; re-enter when 80%+ setup returns
• Net edge must exceed 2× fees — turnover is the goal only when calculated

instruction: Extract commander trading lessons for autonomous execution.

4. Exit winners into spikes; do not give back peak (spike_top_exit).
5. Stand aside when edge is gone — human ended 100% cash; bot should pause entries.
6. Same ticker can lose one trip and win the next (PLUG loss vs USEG energy win) — learn execution per trip.
7. Require entry_quality pass before every trip — human traded cold in February.
8. CALCULATED LOTTERY: 80–97% conviction — ENTER volatile names including PLUG when setup qualifies; EXIT fast on THIS trip if wrong; re-enter when setup returns.

instruction: Extract commander trading lessons for autonomous execution.
input:
Commander IB report 2026-02-06 → 2026-04-27
Return +35.03% | fees −$317 (29% of MTM) | turnover ~$73,789
Task: List what the AI must do BETTER than the human commander.
output:
1. Keep high turnover ONLY when net per dollar traded beats fees.
2. Never allow one trade to lose >3% of session equity (human had −13% trips).
3. Cut losers mechanically on EACH trip — hope-hold was the mistake (CTEV/PLUG/ENVB trips), not the symbols.
4. Exit winners into spikes; do not give back peak (spike_top_exit).
5. Stand aside when edge is gone — human ended 100% cash; bot should pause entries.
6. Same ticker can lose one trip and win the next (PLUG loss vs USEG energy win) — learn execution per trip.
7. Require entry_quality pass before every trip — human traded cold in February.
8. CALCULATED LOTTERY: 80–97% conviction — ENTER volatile names including PLUG when setup qualifies; EXIT fast on THIS trip if wrong; re-enter when setup returns.

instruction: Extract commander trading lessons for autonomous execution.
input:
Commander IB report 2026-02-06 → 2026-04-27
Return +35.03% | fees −$317 (29% of MTM) | turnover ~$73,789
Task: List what the AI must do BETTER than the human commander.
output:
1. Keep high turnover ONLY when net per dollar traded beats fees.
2. Never allow one trade to lose >3% of session equity (human had −13% trips).
3. Cut losers mechanically on EACH trip — hope-hold was the mistake (CTEV/PLUG/ENVB trips), not the symbols.
4. Exit winners into spikes; do not give back peak (spike_top_exit).
5. Stand aside when edge is gone — human ended 100% cash; bot should pause entries.
6. Same ticker can lose one trip and win the next (PLUG loss vs USEG energy win) — learn execution per trip.
7. Require entry_quality pass before every trip — human traded cold in February.
8. CALCULATED LOTTERY: 80–97% conviction — ENTER volatile names including PLUG when setup qualifies; EXIT fast on THIS trip if wrong; re-enter when setup returns.
